title: Solid-State Elastocaloric Cooling Hits Sub-Zero Mark - IEEE Spectrum
description: A new elastocaloric device can freeze water using solid-state tech, offering a greener cooling solution.
bodyText: Prototype uses elastocaloric cooling to achieve -12 °C Laurie Winkless is a New Zealand-based freelance science journalist. The researchers’ “desktop” elastocaloric device stands 0.5 meters tall and 1 meter wide. It achieved a cold-source temperature of -12 ℃ from a room-temperature heat sink at 24 ℃, establishing a temperature lift of 36 ℃.
